headlines.headline: Iran offers to reopen Strait of Hormuz if US ends blockade and war
headlines.summary: Iran has offered to end its control over the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the US lifting its naval blockade and ending the war, according to regional officials. The proposal makes no concessions on Irans nuclear program.

headlines.headline: Europes defense hinges on Ukraine as US commitment wavers
headlines.summary: With doubts growing about Americas commitment to transatlantic allies, Europe must strengthen its common defenses within NATO and the EU. The Economist argues Europe needs Ukraine to fight Russia.

headlines.headline: North Koreas nuclear arsenal outgrowing US missile defenses
headlines.summary: Bloomberg analysis shows North Koreas ascent from rogue state to full-fledged atomic power means it may soon have a globally relevant deterrent, with ICBMs capable of striking the US mainland.

headlines.headline: Nigeria charges six with treason over coup plot against president
headlines.summary: Nigeria has charged six men with treason for an attempted coup against President Bola Tinubu last October, court filings show. The case highlights political instability in Africas most populous nation.

headlines.headline: Germany unveils strategy to build Europes strongest military by 2039
headlines.summary: Germany announced its first stand-alone military strategy, intending to become the strongest conventional army in Europe. Defense spending has hit a 36-year high as Berlin boosts infantry and space programs.

headlines.headline: Magyar's Russian energy phase-out plan for Hungary lacks urgency, analysts warn
headlines.summary: Opposition leader Peter Magyar's proposal to end Hungary's reliance on Russian fossil fuels falls short, risking continued dependence on Moscow under Viktor Orban's government.

headlines.headline: Egypt concludes major military exercises east of Suez, raising Israeli concerns
headlines.summary: Egypt's defense minister oversaw large-scale war drills near the Suez Canal, with Israeli officials expressing concern about the show of force near their border.

headlines.headline: Pentagon signs deal with Google to integrate Gemini AI into defense operations
headlines.summary: The US Defense Department reached an agreement with Google for access to the company's powerful Gemini AI capabilities, expanding military use of artificial intelligence.

headlines.headline: South Africa to deport Robert Mugabe's son over firearm conviction
headlines.summary: Bellarmine Mugabe, son of the late Zimbabwean president, faces deportation from South Africa after being fined $36,000 for illegal possession of a firearm.

headlines.headline: Trump-Merz Clash Reveals US-Europe Divide on Iran Policy
headlines.summary: A diplomatic clash between Trump and German Chancellor Merz highlights growing transatlantic tensions over handling the Iran conflict and security commitments.

headlines.headline: CNN Maps Strait of Hormuz Shipping Disruption
headlines.summary: CNN published detailed visualizations showing how shipping traffic through the strategic Strait of Hormuz has collapsed since the US-Iran conflict began.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Develops Private Air Defense Against Shahed Drones
headlines.summary: Ukraine is building a private air defense network to counter Russian Shahed drone attacks as part of efforts to protect critical infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Trump-Xi Summit to Put Taiwan Independence at Center of Agenda
headlines.summary: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping will meet in Beijing next month for a summit where China is likely to make the issue of Taiwan independence a major part of the agenda. The meeting comes amid heightened tensions over the self-governing island.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan Tops Agenda for Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing
perspectives.summary: The upcoming Trump-Xi summit will focus heavily on Taiwan, with Beijing likely to make the issue central to the agenda. For Taiwan's 23 million people, this may be the most consequential diplomatic meeting of 2026—one they are not invited to attend.

headlines.headline: King Charles State Visit Aims to Repair Strained US-UK Ties
headlines.summary: King Charles' courting of President Trump during his state visit underscores how London is playing a long game to repair frayed relations between the two allies. The visit comes amid deep divisions over the Iran war.

headlines.headline: UAE OPEC Exit Exposes Deepening Saudi Rift
headlines.summary: The UAE's decision to quit OPEC has brought years of tensions with Saudi Arabia into the open, marking a strategic break with Saudi-led oil governance. The move signals a rebalancing of Gulf power dynamics.

perspectives.angle: UAE exit from OPEC risks bitter price war and loss of cartel influence
perspectives.summary: UAE decision to leave OPEC on May 1 will sharply diminish the 65-year-old producer group influence over oil markets, opening door to all-out price war once Iran war ends.

headlines.headline: Iranian Parliament Speaker Says US Blockade Aims to Make Iran Collapse
headlines.summary: Mohammad Baghir Ghalibaf, Iran's Parliamentary Speaker, claimed the US naval blockade of Iranian-linked ships through Hormuz is designed to make Iran "collapse from within."

headlines.headline: Belarus Blocks Russian Conscripts From Leaving, Raising Risks for Ukrainians in Occupied Territories
headlines.summary: Belarus has prohibited Russian conscripts from leaving the country, a move that raises concerns about mobilization and increases risks for Ukrainians in Russian-occupied areas.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Parliament Advances Reforms as Funding Deadlines Loom
headlines.summary: Ukraine's parliament is slowly working through reforms tied to billions in funding from lenders, with deadlines still not met on over a dozen required measures.

headlines.headline: Trump says King Charles does not want Iran to have nuclear weapon
headlines.summary: President Trump introduced the Middle East conflict into a White House state dinner for King Charles, saying the British monarch shares US concerns about Iran obtaining nuclear weapons.

perspectives.angle: King Charles Raises Iran Nuclear Concern at White House State Dinner
perspectives.summary: President Trump said Britain's King Charles does not want Iran to obtain nuclear weapons, bringing Middle East tensions into a state visit aimed at reaffirming US-UK ties amid deep divisions over the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Trump urges Iran to sign deal as US considers extending port blockade
headlines.summary: President Trump discussed extending the US blockade of Iranian ports for months, urging Tehran to get smart and sign an agreement as the conflict drags on.

perspectives.angle: Oil prices surge as US moves to extend Iran port blockade
perspectives.summary: Oil prices extended their rally on reports the US will extend its blockade of Iranian ports, likely prolonging supply disruptions from the Middle East. The move signals no immediate end to the conflict that has constrained global oil flows.

headlines.headline: Putin accuses Ukraine of intensifying attacks on civilian targets
headlines.summary: President Putin claimed Ukraine has stepped up attacks on civilian infrastructure in Russia, citing a drone strike on an oil refinery in the southern city of Tuapse.

perspectives.angle: Putin Accuses Ukraine of Intensifying Attacks on Russian Civilians
perspectives.summary: President Vladimir Putin said Ukraine has stepped up attacks on civilian infrastructure in Russia, citing a recent drone strike on an oil refinery in the southern city of Tuapse.

headlines.headline: Israel quietly expands military control zone in Gaza
headlines.summary: New maps issued by Israel have expanded restricted areas in Gaza, putting thousands of displaced Palestinians inside boundaries the military says it can continue to change.

headlines.headline: Fed chief nominee Warsh clears key Senate hurdle
headlines.summary: Kevin Warsh, Trumps pick to lead the Federal Reserve, cleared a key procedural hurdle in the Senate, paving the way to succeed Jerome Powell next month.

perspectives.angle: Fed Chair Nominee Warsh Set to Clear Key Confirmation Hurdle
perspectives.summary: Kevin Warsh, President Trumps pick to lead the Federal Reserve, is poised to clear a procedural vote on Wednesday, setting the stage for him to succeed Jerome Powell as the White House exerts unprecedented control over the central bank.

headlines.headline: Russia Cancels Military Equipment at Victory Day Parade Citing Ukrainian Threat
headlines.summary: Russia will hold a slimmed-down Victory Day parade on Red Square without tanks or missiles for the first time since the Ukraine war began, citing increased threat of Ukrainian attacks.

perspectives.angle: Russia Scales Down Victory Day Parade Citing Ukrainian Threat
perspectives.summary: Russia announced it will reduce its annual WWII Victory Day parade due to security concerns from Ukrainian drone attacks, marking a significant symbolic shift.

headlines.headline: US Ambassador to Ukraine Steps Down as Peace Talks Stall
headlines.summary: Acting US Ambassador Julie Davis will retire and step down from her post in Kyiv amid a lull in US-brokered ceasefire talks to end Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: US Ambassador to Ukraine Steps Down Amid Stalled Peace Talks
perspectives.summary: Acting US Ambassador Julie Davis will retire and leave her Kyiv post in June, as US-brokered ceasefire negotiations with Russia remain deadlocked.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Pumping Station 1,500 km Inside Russia
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drones hit an oil pumping station near the Ural Mountains, 900 miles inside Russian territory, as Zelenskiy vows to continue extending the range of strikes.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Strikes Russian Shadow Fleet Oil Tanker Near Tuapse
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces hit the sanctioned oil tanker MARQUISE with two kamikaze drones near the Russian port of Tuapse. The attack targets Russia's shadow fleet used to circumvent sanctions.

headlines.headline: Russia Attacks Odesa Port Infrastructure, Damages Hospital
headlines.summary: Russian drone strikes hit port infrastructure in Ukraine's southern Odesa region, damaging residential buildings and a hospital, wounding two people.

headlines.headline: London police declare Golders Green stabbing a terror incident
headlines.summary: British counterterror police have formally classified the stabbing of two Jewish men in Londons Golders Green as a terrorism incident, escalating security concerns for the UK Jewish community.

headlines.headline: Lebanon president demands Israel fully implement ceasefire before talks
headlines.summary: President Joseph Aoun stated that Israel must fully implement the ceasefire agreement before any diplomatic negotiations can proceed, amid ongoing cross-border incidents.

headlines.headline: US Marines train to repel beach invaders in Philippines defense exercise
headlines.summary: US Marines conducted joint exercises with Philippine forces to defend against simulated amphibious invasions, reinforcing regional military partnerships amid Indo-Pacific tensions.

headlines.headline: US-Iran War Cost at $25 Billion So Far, Pentagon Official Tells Congress
headlines.summary: A senior Pentagon official provided the first official cost estimate for the US war in Iran during congressional testimony, revealing $25 billion spent primarily on munitions. The disclosure comes as Defense Secretary Hegseth faces questioning about the conflict for the first time.

perspectives.angle: Hegseth defends Iran war costs before Congress as Democrats question strategy
perspectives.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced skeptical lawmakers in his first congressional appearance since the Iran war began, with Pentagon officials estimating  billion spent so far.

headlines.headline: Supreme Court Strikes Down Louisiana Majority-Black District
headlines.summary: The Supreme Court ruled 6-3 to invalidate Louisiana's second majority-Black congressional district, narrowing the scope of the Voting Rights Act. The decision could impact voting rights litigation nationwide ahead of the 2026 midterms.

perspectives.angle: Supreme Court strikes down Louisianas congressional map in Voting Rights Act ruling
perspectives.summary: The Supreme Court ruled 6-3 along ideological lines to strike down Louisianas congressional map, a decision with significant implications for voting rights and the 2026 midterms.

perspectives.angle: US Supreme Court reinstates Texas electoral map favoring Republicans
perspectives.summary: The conservative-majority Supreme Court reinstated a redrawn Texas electoral map that could flip up to five Democratic-held House seats to Republicans. The ruling overturned a lower court decision that had found the map racially discriminatory.

headlines.headline: EU Loosens State Aid Rules as Iran War Drives Up Energy Costs
headlines.summary: The European Union will allow governments to subsidize companies affected by soaring fuel and fertilizer prices triggered by the Iran war. The move reflects growing economic strain from the Middle East conflict on European industries.

perspectives.angle: German Inflation Jumps as Middle East War Drives Energy Costs Higher
perspectives.summary: German inflation reached its highest level in over two years in April as the Iran-Israel war triggered a surge in energy costs. The data highlights the economic spillover effects of the Middle East conflict on European economies.

headlines.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates in Powell's Final Meeting as Chair
headlines.summary: The Federal Reserve is expected to hold interest rates steady at what may be Jerome Powell's last policy meeting as chair. Kevin Warsh, Trump's nominee to replace Powell, cleared a key Senate committee hurdle on Wednesday.

perspectives.angle: Fed Expected to Hold Rates at Powell's Likely Final Meeting
perspectives.summary: The Federal Reserve is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged at what may be Jerome Powell's last policy meeting as chair, with inflation at 3.3% and economic uncertainty from the Iran war clouding the outlook for rate cuts.

headlines.headline: Two stabbed in London Jewish neighborhood, PM condemns antisemitic attack
headlines.summary: Two people were stabbed in Golders Green, a London neighborhood with large Jewish population, in what UK Prime Minister Starmer called an antisemitic attack amid rising tensions over Middle East war.

perspectives.angle: Two Jewish Men Stabbed in London in Terror Incident
perspectives.summary: London police declared a stabbing attack in Golders Green a terror incident after two Jewish men were wounded amid heightened Middle East tensions.

headlines.headline: Iranian rial crashes to record low as economic strain deepens after war
headlines.summary: Iran currency dropped to a record low of 1,810,000 rials to the US dollar as demand for foreign exchange that built up during six weeks of fighting flows into the open market under the ceasefire.

headlines.headline: US stocks fall as oil prices surge past $4 per gallon nationally
headlines.summary: Wall Street retreated as oil prices continued climbing amid Iran war uncertainty, with national average gas price reaching $4.229 per gallon, the highest since the conflict began.

headlines.headline: Israel President Invites Netanyahu Trial Parties to Discuss Pardon
headlines.summary: Israel's president has invited lawyers for Prime Minister Netanyahu and prosecutors to discuss a potential pardon request, a significant development in the long-running corruption case against the Israeli leader.

headlines.headline: UBS Beats Profit Estimates on Iran War Market Volatility
headlines.summary: Swiss bank UBS reported better-than-expected first-quarter net profit, helped by record trading revenue as market turbulence from the Iran war drove client activity.

perspectives.angle: AstraZeneca Beats Q1 Profit Estimates, Maintains Outlook
perspectives.summary: AstraZeneca beat first-quarter profit expectations and maintained its 2026 forecasts, supported by strong demand for its cancer and rare-disease drugs amid global uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Santander Reports Record Q1 Profit on Strong Spain, Mexico Results
headlines.summary: Spanish bank Santander reported a record first-quarter net profit, with solid performance in Spain and Mexico offsetting weaker results elsewhere, and reaffirmed its 2026 goals.

headlines.headline: European Aluminium Premium Doubles as Iran War Disrupts Middle East Supply
headlines.summary: The premium for aluminium billet in Europe has doubled since the Iran war began due to supply shortages from the Middle East, squeezing manufacturers in construction and transport sectors.

perspectives.angle: Singapore Pulling In Dubai Gold as Middle East War Drags On
perspectives.summary: Gold imports into Singapore from Dubai hit a record last month as wealthy investors seek alternative safe-haven storage hubs amid the prolonged Middle East conflict.

perspectives.angle: BP Profit Doubles to $3.2 Billion on Iran War Oil Trading
perspectives.summary: British oil major BP reported first-quarter profit of $3.2 billion, beating expectations by 20% and marking its highest profit since 2023. The Iran war boosted oil trading results significantly.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy Vows to Keep Extending Range of Ukrainian Strikes Into Russia
headlines.summary: Ukraines president said Kyiv would continue increasing the range of its strikes on Russian territory, posting footage of an attack on a target more than 1,500 km inside Russia.

headlines.headline: Lebanese Soldier Killed in Israeli Airstrike in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: A Lebanese soldier and his brother were killed in an Israeli airstrike on a motorcycle in Bint Jbeil, southern Lebanon. The incident marks another escalation in cross-border violence amid the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict.

perspectives.angle: Hezbollah drone kills Israeli contractor in southern Lebanon
perspectives.summary: An Israeli civilian contractor was killed and his son wounded by an FPV drone strike while operating an excavator in southern Lebanon, where IDF forces are demolishing Hezbollah infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Japan's Growing Muslim Communities Face Resistance to New Mosques
headlines.summary: Muslim leaders in Japan report growing opposition to new mosques, fueled by negative overseas coverage of Islam and online misinformation. The tensions reveal challenges as Japan's Muslim population expands.

perspectives.angle: Japan Faces Tensions Over Growing Muslim Communities and Mosque Construction
perspectives.summary: Resistance to new mosques in Japan is exposing social tensions as the Muslim population grows, highlighting challenges of integration in a traditionally homogeneous society.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Passes Historic Law Protecting Domestic Workers
headlines.summary: Rights groups are hailing Indonesia's new domestic worker protection law as a "new chapter," though activists warn that real change will take time due to entrenched beliefs. The law addresses long-standing labor rights issues.

headlines.headline: IAEA Chief Confirms Iran Highly Enriched Uranium at Isfahan Site
headlines.summary: UN nuclear agency head Rafael Grossi stated that Iran highly enriched uranium is still believed to be at the Isfahan site, amid ongoing concerns about Irans nuclear program during the US-Israel conflict with Iran.

headlines.headline: US Mandates Enhanced Security Checks for Immigration Applicants
headlines.summary: The Trump administration has mandated enhanced security checks for immigration applicants, according to internal guidance sent to USCIS employees, tightening vetting procedures amid the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Standard Chartered and IFC Launch 300M Africa Supply Chain Finance
headlines.summary: The World Bank IFC and Standard Chartered announced a new 300 million risk-sharing facility aimed at boosting supply chain finance across Africa amid global economic uncertainty.

headlines.headline: US Sets Conditions for UN Dues Including China Curbs
headlines.summary: The United States has set specific conditions for releasing billions it owes the UN including cost-cutting measures and moves to counter China influence at the world body.

perspectives.angle: US ties UN dues payment to China curbs, report reveals
perspectives.summary: The United States, owing billions in unpaid fees to the UN, has reportedly attached conditions to its dues payment including measures targeting China, as the organization faces imminent financial collapse.

headlines.headline: Brent Crude Hits One-Month High on Prolonged Hormuz Disruption Fears
headlines.summary: Oil prices rose nearly 3% as Brent hit a one-month high following reports the US will extend its blockade of Iranian ports, likely prolonging supply disruptions from the Middle East.

headlines.headline: Dollar Strengthens as Investors Await Fed Decision Amid Iran War
headlines.summary: The dollar edged higher as markets awaited a key Federal Reserve rate decision in what may be Chair Jerome Powells final meeting, against the backdrop of an Iran conflict with no imminent resolution in sight.

headlines.headline: IRGC seizes wartime power as Irans Supreme Leader role diminishes
headlines.summary: Two months into war with the US and Israel, Irans Revolutionary Guards have consolidated decision-making power, with Mojtaba Khamenei reduced to legitimizing generals decisions rather than issuing directives. The shift may harden Tehrans stance in any renewed talks with Washington.

headlines.headline: Chinese research vessel departs near Japans southwest islands after two weeks
headlines.summary: A Chinese research vessel departed waters near Japans Nansei Islands after a two-week presence, marking the third such incident in a month. Japans Defense Ministry is monitoring increased Chinese maritime activity near its southwestern islands.

headlines.headline: KAI and Bell partner on South Koreas next-generation utility helicopter
headlines.summary: Korea Aerospace Industries and Bell Textron signed a memorandum of understanding to develop South Koreas next-generation high-speed medium utility helicopter. The partnership advances Seouls military modernization amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: EU charges Meta for failing to keep children under 13 off Facebook and Instagram
headlines.summary: The European Commission issued preliminary findings that Meta breached EU digital rules by inadequately preventing children under 13 from accessing Facebook and Instagram, potentially exposing them to age-inappropriate content.

headlines.headline: South Korea Sentences Ex-President Yoon to 7 Years
headlines.summary: Ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol receives a 7-year sentence for resisting arrest and bypassing Cabinet procedures before his martial law declaration, adding to his existing life sentence for rebellion.

headlines.headline: UK Feels Economic Strain From Iran War
headlines.summary: British consumer confidence slumps to two-year low as the Iran war drives price increases, with PM Starmer refusing US requests to use UK bases for strikes on Iranian infrastructure.

headlines.headline: EU Accuses Meta of Failing to Block Underage Users
headlines.summary: The European Union charged Meta with violating digital rules by not preventing children under 13 from accessing Facebook and Instagram, threatening fines up to 6% of global revenue.

headlines.headline: Belarus Frees Polish Journalist in Prisoner Swap With Poland
headlines.summary: Belarus released Andrzej Poczobut, a prominent Polish-Belarusian journalist and activist, as part of a prisoner exchange at the border with Poland, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk announced.

perspectives.angle: Belarus Frees Polish Journalist in Prisoner Swap
perspectives.summary: Belarus released prominent journalist Andrzej Poczobut after five years in prison as part of a 10-person prisoner exchange with Poland, marking a rare diplomatic breakthrough amid ongoing tensions.

headlines.headline: Mali Junta Leader Vows to Neutralize Attackers After Coordinated Offensive
headlines.summary: Mali's military leader Assimi Goita made his first public appearance since insurgents launched coordinated attacks over the weekend, vowing to "neutralize" those responsible as he met with Russia's ambassador.

headlines.headline: Russia as Economic Lifeline for Iran amid Hormuz Blockade
headlines.summary: Iranian FM Araghchi visited St Petersburg to discuss sanctions and Hormuz with Putin, as Tehran explores railway and Caspian trade networks to bypass Gulf disruptions. Experts say Moscow could offer a lifeline but logistical issues and costs offer little long-term incentive.

headlines.headline: Europe Named Fastest-Warming Continent in UN Climate Report
headlines.summary: World Meteorological Organization reports Europe experienced above-average temperatures in 2025, with record marine heatwaves and wildfires burning over 1 million hectares. Nearly 70% of European rivers recorded below-average annual flows.

headlines.headline: China Suspends New Autonomous Driving Permits After Baidu Incident
headlines.summary: Beijing halted issuance of new robotaxi licenses after about 200 driverless vehicles suddenly stopped in Wuhan in March. The move clouds the outlook for Chinas autonomous vehicle sector.

headlines.headline: Mexico Opens Investigation into Unauthorized CIA Agents in Country
headlines.summary: Mexicos attorney general announced a full probe into two US CIA agents who died in a car crash while operating in the country without authorization. The incident raises questions about US intelligence activities in Mexico.

headlines.headline: Australian State Launches First Gas Tender in a Decade
headlines.summary: New South Wales opened its first natural gas exploration tender in ten years amid supply crunch from Middle East war. Entry fees were slashed from A$50,000 to A$1,000 to encourage participation. Queensland also pushed for fast-track approval of first new oil project in 50 years.

headlines.headline: World Bank Warns Energy Prices Could Surge 24% in 2026 Due to Iran War
headlines.summary: The World Bank forecasts energy prices will reach their highest level since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, driven by Middle East war disruptions even in the most optimistic scenario where conflict subsides by May.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan Oil Import Costs Surge 167% Since Iran War Began
perspectives.summary: Pakistan's oil import bill has jumped to $800M per week since the Iran conflict started, forcing emergency conservation measures and straining the economy.

headlines.headline: Five Million Children in Darfur Face Extreme Hunger and Violence as Sudan War Enters Fourth Year
headlines.summary: UNICEF issued an emergency warning over the humanitarian crisis in Sudan's Darfur region, where civil war has created devastating conditions for children facing extreme deprivation, hunger, and violence.

perspectives.angle: UN warns five million children in Darfur face extreme deprivation as Sudan war enters fourth year
perspectives.summary: UNICEF issued an emergency warning over the humanitarian crisis in Sudans Darfur region, where five million children face extreme hunger, violence and displacement as the civil war continues into its fourth year.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Coast Guard Chief Visits Disputed South China Sea Islet Despite Vietnam Protests
headlines.summary: Taiwan's Coast Guard minister visited a second Taiwan-controlled islet deep in the South China Sea, dismissing Vietnam's complaints and asserting Taiwan's presence in the contested waterway amid regional tensions.

perspectives.angle: China Denounces Japan and EU Over South China Sea at UN
perspectives.summary: China accused Japan of provocative behavior in the Taiwan Strait and planning military expansion after Tokyo and the EU raised concerns about the South China Sea at a UN Security Council meeting.

headlines.headline: Philippines Defense Chief Says Iran War Won't Distract US from Indo-Pacific
headlines.summary: The Philippine defense secretary expressed confidence that US military capabilities in the Indo-Pacific remain strong despite Middle East commitments, while warning China could attempt to exploit any perceived opening.

perspectives.angle: Philippines Confident US Commitment to Indo-Pacific Amid Iran War
perspectives.summary: The Philippine defense secretary said Manila is not worried about reduced US deterrence in the Indo-Pacific due to the Middle East conflict, though he warned China could try to exploit any perceived opening.

headlines.headline: Purdue Pharma sentenced to $5.5B, OxyContin maker to dissolve
headlines.summary: OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma received a $5.5 billion sentence after pleading guilty to deceiving regulators and paying kickbacks to doctors. The sentencing paves the way for the companys dissolution and opioid settlements.

headlines.headline: Iran IRGC tightens grip as war strains economy and blunts Supreme Leader role
headlines.summary: Wartime pressure has concentrated power in Iran into a narrower, harder-line inner circle as the IRGC seizes control, blunting the Supreme Leaders role. Two months into the US-Israel war, the Revolutionary Guards dominance grows amid economic collapse.

headlines.headline: US and NATO face frustrating marriage crisis, Belgian defense minister says
headlines.summary: Belgian Defense Minister Theo Francken described strained transatlantic relations as a marriage crisis, urging NATO unity while acknowledging growing tensions in the alliance during the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Sexual violence in Sudan creates mental health crisis, UN warns
headlines.summary: The WHO reports a mental health emergency in Sudan as sexual violence affects tens of thousands of women and girls amid the ongoing civil war. Access to healthcare is severely limited, with survivors facing stigma and lack of trained medical staff.

perspectives.angle: Sexual Violence in Sudan Triggers Mental Health Crisis, UN Warns
perspectives.summary: The United Nations reports widespread sexual violence in Sudan is causing a severe mental health crisis, as conflict between the military and RSF paramilitary continues.

headlines.headline: Jury deliberates in trial of alleged ISIS militant charged in Kabul airport bombing
headlines.summary: Federal jurors in Virginia are weighing charges against Mohammad Sharifullah, accused of helping plan the 2021 Abbey Gate suicide bombing that killed 13 US service members and 160 Afghans during the chaotic withdrawal from Afghanistan.

headlines.headline: Trump says Iran in collapse, wants US to reopen Hormuz Strait
headlines.summary: President Trump stated that Iran has said it is in a state of collapse and wants the US to open the Strait of Hormuz while sorting out its leadership. The comment suggests Tehran is seeking an off-ramp from the prolonged conflict.

perspectives.angle: Trump Says Iran Wants US to Open Hormuz Strait as Soon as Possible
perspectives.summary: President Trump stated that Iran has communicated it is in a "state of collapse" and wants the United States to open the Strait of Hormuz while it sorts out its leadership situation amid ongoing peace talks.

headlines.headline: US imposes sanctions on 35 entities for Iran sanctions evasion
headlines.summary: The US government ratcheted up pressure on Tehran, sanctioning 35 entities and individuals for roles in Iran shadow banking sector, and threatened sanctions against banks dealing with Chinese teapot refineries paying Hormuz tolls.

perspectives.angle: US imposes sanctions on 35 entities for aiding Iran's sanctions evasion network
perspectives.summary: The US government escalated pressure on Tehran by sanctioning 35 individuals and entities involved in Iran's shadow banking sector, and threatened sanctions against Chinese "teapot" refineries paying tolls for Hormuz shipments.

headlines.headline: US bond markets split as Iran war tests Federal Reserve outlook
headlines.summary: The Middle East conflict has splintered the US fixed-income market, with corporate credit investors behaving as if the Iran war is over while Treasury holders remain focused on inflation risks that could limit Fed easing.

perspectives.angle: US Bond Markets Split as Middle East Conflict Tests Fed Policy Outlook
perspectives.summary: The Iran war has fractured US fixed-income markets, with corporate credit investors pricing in peace while Treasury holders remain focused on inflation risks that could limit Federal Reserve easing.

perspectives.angle: Markets See Through Iran War as Permacrisis Becomes Norm
perspectives.summary: Record-high stocks amid a geopolitical shock puzzles investors. The dissonance suggests we are now stuck in a world of permanent crisis where markets exploit volatility.

headlines.headline: First Japan-linked tanker crosses Hormuz since Iran war began
headlines.summary: A Panama-flagged tanker carrying 2 million barrels of Saudi oil crossed the Strait of Hormuz, becoming the first Japan-linked crude tanker to do so since the conflict began, a sign of tentative normalization.

perspectives.angle: First Japanese Oil Tanker Crosses Hormuz Since Iran War Began
perspectives.summary: The Idemitsu Maru, carrying 2 million barrels of Saudi crude, became the first Japan-linked tanker to successfully navigate the blockaded Strait of Hormuz since the conflict started.

headlines.headline: Chinese Scientists Pioneer Converting Carbon Dioxide Into Jet Fuel
headlines.summary: A Chinese research team has developed a method to synthesize jet fuel from carbon dioxide as soaring oil prices from the Iran conflict drive interest in alternative aviation fuels.

headlines.headline: China Condemns Japan Over Neo-Militarism Amid Regional Tensions
headlines.summary: Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson calls for countries to resist Japan's reckless moves toward neo-militarism as Tokyo expands defense capabilities.

headlines.headline: King Charles Addresses Congress in Rare Speech
headlines.summary: King Charles III became only the second British monarch to address the U.S. Congress, urging American lawmakers to maintain global leadership and reject isolationism. The speech came amid heightened geopolitical tensions between the United States and Iran.

perspectives.angle: King Charles urges unyielding resolve for Ukraine in historic Congress speech
perspectives.summary: The British monarch addressed the US Congress emphasizing continued support for Ukraine and NATO unity, using symbolic soft power to reinforce transatlantic commitment during a critical period for Western alliances.

headlines.headline: Former FBI Director James Comey Indicted Over Seashell Photo
headlines.summary: James Comey faces criminal charges for a second time after posting an Instagram photo of seashells arranged as "86 47," which officials interpreted as a threat against President Trump. The indictment marks an unprecedented escalation in tensions between the former FBI director and the Trump administration.

perspectives.angle: Ex-FBI Director Comey indicted again over seashell photo seen as Trump threat
perspectives.summary: James Comey faces a second indictment over a social media post showing seashells arranged in a pattern that officials interpreted as a potential threat against President Trump.

headlines.headline: SK Group Chairman Urges South Korea-Japan Economic Integration
headlines.summary: SK Group Chairman Choi Tae-won called for South Korea to pursue economic integration with Japan and lead a broader Asian economic alliance to compete in the AI era. The remarks come as U.S.-China rivalry intensifies in technology development.

headlines.headline: North Korea ICBMs Can Strike US Mainland, Pentagon Official Warns
headlines.summary: A senior US defense official confirmed North Korea has the capability to strike the US mainland with intercontinental ballistic missiles, underscoring the growing ballistic missile threat amid Pyongyang's diplomatic outreach to Trump.

headlines.headline: Global Military Spending Hits Record $4 Trillion
headlines.summary: Global defense expenditure reached a historic high of $4 trillion, with the United States, China, and Russia accounting for more than half of total spending, according to new research reflecting intensified great power competition.

headlines.headline: NATO weaknesses exposed by Iran war, analysis finds
headlines.summary: A Politico EU analysis identifies five key ways the Iran conflict reveals NATO is unprepared to fight Russia, from munitions depletion to alliance coordination gaps.

headlines.headline: Hormuz shipping traffic remains muted as US-Iran talks stall
headlines.summary: Only 7 ships crossed the Strait in 24 hours, a fraction of the 140 daily passages before the war. Data shows Iran has moved oil through the US blockade while negotiations remain deadlocked.

headlines.headline: Jury deliberates in trial of IS militant over Kabul airport bombing
headlines.summary: A Virginia jury began deliberations in the trial of Mohammad Sharifullah, an alleged Islamic State militant charged with helping plan the 2021 Abbey Gate suicide bombing that killed 13 US service members and 160 Afghans during the US withdrawal from Afghanistan.

headlines.headline: Trump approval rating sinks to record low as Iran war drives cost-of-living concerns
headlines.summary: President Trump's approval rating has fallen to the lowest level of his second term, with Americans citing rising gasoline prices and economic strain from the Iran war as key factors in their dissatisfaction.

perspectives.angle: MAGA Rift Deepens as Trump Faces Media Backlash Over Iran War
perspectives.summary: Prominent MAGA voices including Tucker Carlson and Megyn Kelly are breaking with Trump over his handling of the Iran war, signaling a growing divide within his base.

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan holds rates steady but three board members dissent, call for hike
headlines.summary: The BOJ kept interest rates unchanged but three of nine board members proposed hiking borrowing costs, signaling concern over inflationary pressures from the Middle East conflict affecting Japan's economy.

headlines.headline: Russian shadow fleet undeterred by UK threats, nearly 100 ships cross British waters
headlines.summary: A Reuters analysis shows that Prime Minister Keir Starmer's decision to let the British military board Russian shadow fleet ships has had no clear impact, with nearly 100 vessels still passing through UK waters.

headlines.headline: Ukraine shuts down arms network supplying weapons to pro-Russian figures including Steven Seagal
headlines.summary: Ukrainian authorities dismantled an arms trafficking network that funneled weapons from occupied territories and Slovakia to pro-Russian figures, including actor Steven Seagal.

headlines.headline: McConnell criticizes Pentagon for withholding M in Ukraine aid
headlines.summary: Former Senate GOP Leader Mitch McConnell criticized the Pentagon for sitting on  million in congressionally approved Ukraine aid, highlighting tensions over military support.

headlines.headline: Senate rejects bid to curb Trump military action in Cuba
headlines.summary: The Senate voted down legislation that would have barred US military action against Cuba without congressional approval, maintaining presidential war powers flexibility.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Confronts Israel Over Purchase of Russian-Stolen Grain
headlines.summary: President Zelenskiy condemned Israel for buying grain from occupied Ukrainian territory that he says was stolen by Russia, threatening sanctions and sparking a diplomatic row.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine asks Israel to seize vessel carrying stolen grain
perspectives.summary: Kyiv has formally requested Israel to seize a ship carrying grain allegedly stolen from occupied Ukrainian territories, as diplomatic tensions between Ukraine and Israel escalate.

headlines.headline: Major US Companies Project Resilience Despite Mounting Iran War Risks
headlines.summary: Top American firms from GM to Coca-Cola are reassuring investors they can weather Iran war fallout, even as surging fuel and packaging costs threaten profit margins.

headlines.headline: Drone Diplomacy Wins Ukraine Allies, But Now It Must Deliver
headlines.summary: Zelenskiy has leveraged Ukraine drone warfare expertise into diplomatic deals across Middle East and Europe, using military prowess to boost Kyiv international clout.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Drone Strike Sparks Fire at Russian Tuapse Refinery
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack caused a major fire at a Russian oil refinery in Tuapse, with Putin citing the incident as evidence of intensified Ukrainian attacks on civilian infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Oil Tanker in Black Sea
perspectives.summary: Ukraine resumed drone attacks against Russian-trading tankers, hitting a laden product tanker with kamikaze drones in the Black Sea. The attack highlights Ukraine's ongoing campaign against Russian shipping and energy infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Another Russian Oil Refinery Burns After Ukrainian Drone Attack
perspectives.summary: A Russian oil facility in the Perm region caught fire following a reported Ukrainian drone attack. President Zelenskyy touted Ukraine's expanding drone reach as attacks on Russian energy infrastructure continue.

headlines.headline: Russian Superyacht Allowed Through Blocked Hormuz Strait
headlines.summary: A superyacht owned by sanctioned Russian billionaire Alexey Mordashov transited the blockaded Strait of Hormuz after neither Iran nor the US objected, highlighting selective enforcement of the wartime closure.

headlines.headline: US Military Boards Cargo Ship During Iran Blockade, Releases It
headlines.summary: The US military boarded a commercial vessel suspected of heading to Iran during the ongoing blockade but released it after inspection, marking a departure from previous interdictions.

headlines.headline: Peace Talks at Impasse as Trump Rejects Latest Iran Proposal
headlines.summary: President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with Iran latest ceasefire proposal, saying Tehran informed the US it is in a state of collapse and figuring out its leadership situation.

perspectives.angle: German Chancellor Merz Says Iran Humiliating US in Stalled Peace Talks
perspectives.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized the lack of progress in US-Iran negotiations, saying Tehran is making American officials travel to Pakistan and leave without results while the Iranian leadership continues to dictate terms.

headlines.headline: China Orders Maritime Security Research as Hormuz Crisis Exposes Trade Risks
headlines.summary: Beijing launches comprehensive research agenda on safeguarding port access and critical mineral investments amid rising threats to global shipping lanes. The move highlights China vulnerability to Middle East disruptions.

headlines.headline: IDF Destroys Two Massive Hezbollah Attack Tunnels Built with Iranian Guidance
headlines.summary: Israel Defense Forces demolished two major Hezbollah tunnels in southern Lebanon, spanning 2 km and reaching depths of 25 meters, using 450 tons of explosives. The tunnels were constructed with direct Iranian involvement.

perspectives.angle: IDF Destroys Over 1,000 Hezbollah Sites in Southern Lebanon
perspectives.summary: Israeli forces dismantled more than 1,000 Hezbollah infrastructure sites and uncovered weapons caches including anti-tank missiles and mortar shells. The operations come amid escalating tensions with Hezbollah rejecting ceasefire terms.

headlines.headline: US Ambassador to Ukraine to Resign Amid Tensions with Trump
headlines.summary: Julie Davis, who has served as charge daffaires at the US Embassy in Kyiv since May 2025, has clashed with President Trump over Ukraine policy and is reportedly preparing to resign.

headlines.headline: China Testing Truck-Mounted Nuclear Reactor for AI Data Centers
headlines.summary: Chinese scientists are developing a prototype mobile nuclear power bank that could generate up to 10MW of energy, potentially ending battery anxiety for power-hungry AI data centers.

perspectives.angle: China Tests Truck-Mounted Nuclear Reactor for AI Data Centers
perspectives.summary: Chinese scientists are testing a prototype 10-megawatt vehicle-mounted nuclear reactor that could power medium-sized AI data centers. The "nuclear power bank" offers decades-long operation without recharging and could serve remote regions, ships, and space systems.

headlines.headline: Iranian Revolutionary Guards seize wartime power, diminishing Supreme Leader's authority
headlines.summary: Two months into the war with the US and Israel, Iran no longer has a single undisputed clerical arbiter at the pinnacle of power—a significant break from the Islamic Republic's structure that could be hardening Tehran's stance in talks with Washington.

perspectives.angle: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Seize Wartime Power, Blunting Supreme Leader's Role
perspectives.summary: Two months into the war with the US and Israel, Iran no longer has a single undisputed clerical arbiter at the pinnacle of power, a break with the past that may be hardening Tehran's stance in negotiations with Washington.

headlines.headline: US and Iran clash at UN after Tehran appointed to nuclear non-proliferation role
headlines.summary: The United States and Iran clashed at the United Nations over Tehran's nuclear program after Iran was selected as one of the vice presidents at a month-long conference reviewing the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty.

perspectives.angle: US and Iran Clash at UN as Tehran Gets Nuclear Non-Proliferation Role
perspectives.summary: The United States and Iran exchanged sharp accusations at the opening of a month-long UN conference reviewing the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty, with tensions heightened by Iran's election as a vice president of the proceedings.

headlines.headline: Over 1,100 Ukrainians Accused of Betrayal, One in Five Are Minors
headlines.summary: A Reuters investigation reveals that since Russia's full-scale invasion, more than 1,100 Ukrainians have been accused of betrayal through arson, terrorism or sabotage, with 20% being minors. The case of one teenager illustrates how children can be both perpetrator and victim.

headlines.headline: Groups Petition US Trade Agency to Combat Forced Labor Imports
headlines.summary: Pro-tariff and human rights groups asked the Trump administration to impose new import bans, duties and quotas targeting forced labor practices in China and other countries, pressing trading partners to adopt US-style enforcement.

headlines.headline: Ukraine reveals details of Russia's new S-71K cruise missile
headlines.summary: Ukrainian military intelligence disclosed specifications of Russia's new air-launched S-71K "Kovyor" cruise missile, including its design and foreign components. The revelation comes as Kyiv continues to expose Moscow's weapons supply chains.

headlines.headline: German defense minister says Ukraine partnership benefits Germany
headlines.summary: German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius highlighted how Germany benefits from defense cooperation with Ukraine, noting Kyiv's rapid development of battlefield-tested technologies that enhance German military capabilities.

headlines.headline: UK borrowing costs hit highest level since 2008
headlines.summary: UK government borrowing costs closed at their highest level since 2008, driven by economic uncertainty and fiscal concerns under Prime Minister Keir Starmer's government amid global market volatility from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Armenia and Azerbaijan maintain high military spending despite peace deal
headlines.summary: Despite agreeing to a provisional peace agreement last August, both Armenia and Azerbaijan continue to maintain some of the world's highest military spending as a percentage of GDP, reflecting an ongoing "peace-through-strength" approach.

headlines.headline: US Intelligence Studies How Iran Would React to Trump Declaring Victory
headlines.summary: US spy agencies are examining scenarios where President Trump declares a unilateral victory in the two-month-old Iran war, which has killed thousands and become a political liability for the White House.

headlines.headline: Purdue Pharma Criminal Sentence Could Mark Final Step Before Dissolution
headlines.summary: OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma faces a $225 million criminal sentence, potentially the last legal step before the company dissolves over its role in the opioid crisis that killed hundreds of thousands.

headlines.headline: US Soldier Charged with Using Classified Intel to Win K on Polymarket
headlines.summary: A US special forces soldier pleaded not guilty to charges of using classified information about the mission to capture Venezuela's Maduro to win over ,000 on prediction market Polymarket. The case raises concerns about insider trading on prediction platforms.

headlines.headline: Google Signs Classified AI Deal With Pentagon for Military Applications
headlines.summary: Alphabet's Google joined a growing list of technology firms signing deals with the U.S. Department of Defense to use AI models for classified work. The move marks a significant expansion of tech-military collaboration amid the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Gulf Leaders Hold Emergency Meeting in Saudi Arabia on Iran War Crisis
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia hosted an emergency consultative meeting with other Persian Gulf leaders in Jeddah to discuss the ongoing Iran conflict and its regional implications. The summit reflects growing coordination among Gulf states amid the Hormuz standoff.

headlines.headline: EU Trade Chief Resigns After Clashing Over Hasty Trump Deal
headlines.summary: Sabine Weyand, who led the European Commission's trade department for seven years, is leaving after clashing with superiors over a hastily agreed trade deal with U.S. President Donald Trump. The departure highlights tensions in EU-US trade relations.

headlines.headline: US Military Expands Secret Boat Strike Operations with More Aircraft
headlines.summary: The U.S. military has quietly increased the number of secret fixed-wing attack aircraft and armed MQ-9 Reaper drones for boat strike missions in the Iran conflict, according to officials. The expansion reflects the ongoing maritime dimension of the Hormuz standoff.

headlines.headline: Israel Rejects Russian Grain Theft Claims as Ukraine Threatens Sanctions
headlines.summary: Foreign Minister Saar accuses Zelensky of "Twitter diplomacy" after Ukrainian president claims Israel "cannot be unaware" that Russian grain cargo at Haifa port came from occupied territory.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine warns Israel of diplomatic crisis over ship carrying alleged stolen grain
perspectives.summary: Ukraine has warned of a potential diplomatic and legal crisis with Israel if the vessel Panormitis, carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukraine, docks at Haifa port.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Questions Lebanon Peacekeeping Mission as Casualties Mount
headlines.summary: Calls are growing in Indonesia to scale back or end UN peacekeeping contributions in Lebanon after casualties increased amid regional conflict escalation.

headlines.headline: France Demands Israel Remove All Gaza Aid Barriers
headlines.summary: France called on Israel to remove all restrictions on humanitarian aid to Gaza, citing dire conditions as the UN warns of worsening humanitarian crisis.

headlines.headline: Hong Kong Exports Surge 35.8% on AI Electronics Demand
headlines.summary: Hong Kong exports rose at the sharpest rate in over five years, up nearly 36% year-on-year in March, driven by strong demand for AI-related electronics.

headlines.headline: European Shares Subdued as US-Iran Talks Stall
headlines.summary: European stocks were muted as investors weighed a diplomatic impasse in US-Iran negotiations while bracing for central bank meetings and corporate earnings.

perspectives.angle: Dollar eases as US-Iran talks stall ahead of Powell's final Fed meeting
perspectives.summary: The US dollar weakened against the euro as diplomatic impasse in US-Iran negotiations weighed on markets. Investors are also watching for Jerome Powell's expected final Federal Reserve meeting this week.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Rise as Hormuz Standoff Continues
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped over 3% as stalled efforts to end the Iran war kept the Strait of Hormuz largely closed, though UAE's OPEC exit trimmed gains.

headlines.headline: Meta-Manus AI Deal Unwinding Would Be Complex and Time-Consuming, Analysts Say
headlines.summary: Beijing blocked Meta's $2 billion acquisition of Chinese AI startup Manus, but unraveling the deal will be difficult given integration progress over four months. Manus staff have moved into Meta offices and technology has been absorbed into Meta's ecosystem.

headlines.headline: UN General Assembly President Calls for Cross-Regional Alliance to Support UN Amid Iran and Gaza Wars
headlines.summary: UNGA President Annalena Baerbock urged a cross-regional alliance to strengthen the United Nations as it navigates simultaneous crises from the Iran and Gaza conflicts.

headlines.headline: US Air Force Funds Final Development of Beehive Industries' Frenzy 8 Engine for Drone Systems
headlines.summary: The USAF awarded $29.7 million to complete development of the additively manufactured 200-lb thrust Frenzy 8 engine, part of a strategy to develop low-cost disposable jet engines for unmanned aerial systems and standoff weapons.

headlines.headline: Euro Zone Inflation Expectations Surge on Iran War
headlines.summary: Euro zone consumers have sharply raised inflation expectations due to the Iran war fallout, while economic growth is faltering—creating a policy dilemma for the European Central Bank.

headlines.headline: Iranian Oil Tankers Cluster Near Chabahar as U.S. Blockade Tightens
headlines.summary: At least six Iranian tankers laden with oil are loitering near Chabahar port, caught between the Strait of Hormuz and the U.S. naval blockade line. The cluster suggests Iran continues loading oil but the U.S. interception is working to delay exports.

headlines.headline: Ex-Mossad Chief Pardo Condemns West Bank Violence as "Existential Threat" to Israel
headlines.summary: Former Mossad director Tamir Pardo said he was "ashamed to be Jewish" over settler violence in the West Bank, comparing it to Holocaust-era tactics. He warned the crackdown risks civil strife and poses an existential threat to Israel.

headlines.headline: Australia Invests $1.7B in Second HIMARS Regiment for Long-Range Strike
headlines.summary: Australia will spend AUD2.3 billion to establish a second long-range fires regiment equipped with HIMARS launchers and Precision Strike Missiles, capable of hitting targets up to 500 km away, extendable beyond 1,000 km with future PrSM increments.

headlines.headline: Japan Ends Decades-Long Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports to Allies
headlines.summary: Japan has abolished its 1967 ban on lethal weapons exports, opening sales to 17 defense partner countries. The move includes a $6.5B frigate deal with Australia and potential submarine sales to Indonesia, driven by concerns over China and North Korea.

headlines.headline: Europe Faces Jet Fuel Supply Crunch as Middle East Imports Stop
headlines.summary: Europe's jet fuel imports from the Middle East have dried up due to the Iran war disrupting supplies, raising concerns of a shortage before the peak summer travel season. Sweden has already warned of potential shortages.

perspectives.angle: Vietnam gas giant shifts LPG imports from Middle East to US amid Iran war
perspectives.summary: PetroVietnam Gas will import 66,000 tonnes of cooking fuel from US in May versus 44,000 from Middle East, as Iran war disrupts Hormuz Strait and forces Asian buyers to find new suppliers.

headlines.headline: German Chancellor Merz Says Iran Is 'Humiliating' the US
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized the Trump administration's handling of the Iran war, saying Tehran is making US officials travel to Pakistan for talks without results, as European frustration with the conflict grows.

perspectives.angle: German Chancellor Says US Being Humiliated by Iran Negotiations
perspectives.summary: Friedrich Merz criticized US handling of Iran negotiations, saying Tehran is negotiating very skillfully and that the US is being humiliated in the conflict. The comments highlight growing European concerns about transatlantic coordination.

headlines.headline: US Army Launches Sword 26 Exercise to Execute NATO War Plans
headlines.summary: The US Army in Europe kicked off Sword 26, a new training program across eight countries with 15,500 troops aimed at implementing NATO's regional defense plans for the High North and eastern flank, replacing the Defender exercise series.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Official: US Has No Defense Against Hypersonic Weapons
headlines.summary: A senior Pentagon official told Congress the US currently has no defense against hypersonic weapons or advanced cruise missiles, as Trump's $185 billion Golden Dome missile shield program faces scrutiny. China cited as 'pacing competitor'.

headlines.headline: Gulf leaders hold emergency summit in Jeddah on Iran conflict
headlines.summary: Gulf leaders are convening an emergency consultative summit in Jeddah to discuss regional tensions, focusing on the Iran conflict, maritime security, and mediation efforts between Washington and Tehran.

headlines.headline: Bahrain sentences five to life for plotting terrorist acts with Iran
headlines.summary: A Bahraini court sentenced five people to life imprisonment for plotting terrorist and hostile acts with Iran, highlighting ongoing regional tensions and Iranian influence operations in Gulf states.

headlines.headline: Gulf stock markets rise despite lingering geopolitical tensions
headlines.summary: Major Gulf stock markets traded higher on Tuesday amid corporate earnings announcements, though investors remained cautious about ongoing regional tensions from the Iran conflict.

perspectives.angle: Gulf Stock Markets Edge Higher Despite Stalled Iran Diplomacy
perspectives.summary: Most Gulf equity markets eked out small gains despite fading hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the US-Iran conflict. Hardening positions on both sides underscore the difficulty of reaching a negotiated settlement.

headlines.headline: Reuters investigation reveals Ukrainian teens accused of betraying country
headlines.summary: More than 1,100 Ukrainians have been accused of betraying their country since Russia's invasion, with one in five being minors. The investigation examines how children can be seen as both perpetrators and victims.

headlines.headline: El Nino weather pattern threatens global energy markets
headlines.summary: Major meteorological services forecast a strong El Nino pattern from next month, expected to impact global temperatures and rainfall trends while spurring shifts in coal and natural gas consumption across key regions.

headlines.headline: Media freedom under sustained attack across EU, report finds
headlines.summary: Journalists face rising threats while media ownership is concentrated in fewer hands, a leading civil liberties group warns. Public trust in media has dropped significantly across European Union member states.

headlines.headline: Republicans brace for brutal midterms as Trump popularity slips
headlines.summary: President Trump's approval ratings are down, gas prices are up, and congressional Republicans are heading to the exits in record numbers while Democratic enthusiasm rises ahead of November midterm elections.

headlines.headline: Israel Establishes Buffer Zone in Southern Lebanon, Displacing Civilians
headlines.summary: Israeli forces have created a "Yellow Line" buffer zone south of the Litani River in southern Lebanon, forcing residents to flee and raising concerns of long-term displacement amid ongoing conflict with Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: Hundreds of Google Employees Protest Pentagon AI Collaboration
headlines.summary: Google staff signed an open letter urging CEO Sundar Pichai not to allow AI tools for classified Pentagon work, warning of potential misuse in lethal autonomous weapons and surveillance.

perspectives.angle: Google employees petition CEO to refuse classified AI work with Pentagon
perspectives.summary: Hundreds of Google AI researchers have signed a letter urging CEO Sundar Pichai to refuse classified military AI work, reigniting debates about tech company involvement in defense projects.

headlines.headline: Russia to Host International Security Forum with 180+ Foreign Delegations
headlines.summary: Moscow will host its first International Security Forum in late May, bringing together security council secretaries, intelligence chiefs, and defense industry representatives from over 180 countries to showcase Russian military technology.

headlines.headline: China Blocks Meta's $2B Acquisition of AI Startup Manus
headlines.summary: China's National Development and Reform Commission blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, citing national security concerns. The move signals Beijing's tighter control over advanced technology transfers amid U.S.-China AI rivalry.

perspectives.angle: China Blocks Meta From Acquiring AI Startup Manus
perspectives.summary: Beijing tightened scrutiny of AI industry amid intensifying geopolitical rivalry with the US over technology, moving to block Meta from acquiring the AI startup.

headlines.headline: Australia to Tax Meta, Google, TikTok to Fund Journalism
headlines.summary: Australia proposed legislation to tax digital giants Meta, Google and TikTok unless they negotiate deals to pay local media outlets for news content. The News Bargaining Incentive would charge platforms that refuse to strike voluntary deals.

headlines.headline: UK PM Starmer Faces Parliamentary Probe Over Mandelson Appointment
headlines.summary: Britain's parliament will vote on an inquiry into Prime Minister Keir Starmer over whether he misled the House of Commons about the appointment of Peter Mandelson as U.K. ambassador to the U.S., amid questions about Mandelson's past ties.

headlines.headline: Moodys Upgrades China Outlook to Stable on Economic Resilience
headlines.summary: Moodys revised Chinas outlook from negative to stable, citing resilient economic and fiscal strength despite domestic pressures and ongoing trade and geopolitical challenges.

perspectives.angle: Moody's Upgrades China Outlook to Stable, Cites Economic Resilience
perspectives.summary: Credit ratings agency Moody's revised China's outlook from negative to stable, pointing to resilient economic and fiscal strength despite ongoing trade pressures and geopolitical challenges.

headlines.headline: Iran War Fertilizer Squeeze Threatens Global Grain Harvests
headlines.summary: Farmers worldwide face a second fertilizer price surge in four years due to the Iran war. With grain prices too low to cushion the blow, many are reconsidering planting plans, risking global food production.

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan Holds Rates as Three Board Members Push for Hike
headlines.summary: The BOJ kept interest rates steady but three of nine board members proposed raising borrowing costs, reflecting concerns over inflationary pressures from the Middle East conflict.

perspectives.angle: Bank of Japan holds rates steady in split vote as Iran war fuels inflation concerns
perspectives.summary: The BOJ kept its benchmark rate at 0.75% but three of nine board members proposed a hike, signaling growing concern over inflationary pressures from Middle East conflict disrupting energy markets.

headlines.headline: North Korea Executions Doubled During COVID Pandemic
headlines.summary: A new report shows executions and death sentences more than doubled in North Korea after border closures in 2020, with public killings surging for offenses like consuming South Korean media. The regime appears to be intensifying state violence to suppress growing internal discontent.

headlines.headline: South Korea Calls North Korea Most Pressing Nuclear Challenge
headlines.summary: At the NPT review conference in New York, South Korea's nuclear envoy emphasized North Korea's unique threat to nonproliferation and urged Russia to end military cooperation with Pyongyang. Seoul signaled a shift away from CVID toward a phased denuclearization approach.

headlines.headline: Iran revises school strike death toll to 155, including 120 children
headlines.summary: Iran reported 73 boys and 47 girls killed in a February 28 strike on an elementary school in Minab, down from earlier reports of over 175 dead. A US Tomahawk missile hit the school due to a targeting mistake, according to a preliminary military investigation.

headlines.headline: Iran's economy devastated by US-Israel strikes, millions of jobs at risk
headlines.summary: US and Israeli airstrikes damaged 20,000 factories (20% of Iran production). Over 1 million jobs lost directly, with 10-12 million more at risk. Steel and petrochemical sectors crippled. Food prices have surged 68-75% as blockade chokes imports.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Rivals Announce Alliance to Challenge Israeli Government
headlines.summary: Two of Benjamin Netanyahu's top rivals announced they would join forces in upcoming elections to challenge his coalition, focusing on domestic issues including military conscription for ultra-Orthodox Israelis.

perspectives.angle: Israeli Opposition Takes Cues from Hungary to Challenge Netanyahu
perspectives.summary: Two Israeli opposition figures have joined forces, taking inspiration from Hungary where long-time leader Viktor Orban was ousted. The move signals a new unified front against Netanyahu's leadership.

headlines.headline: Lithuania Charges 13 People in GRU-Linked Assassination Attempts
headlines.summary: Lithuanian authorities charged 13 individuals from multiple countries with two attempted murders in Vilnius tied to Russia's GRU military intelligence agency, marking a significant escalation in Russian intelligence operations targeting opponents abroad.

perspectives.angle: Lithuania charges 13 people with attempted murders linked to Russian GRU
perspectives.summary: Lithuania has charged 13 individuals from multiple countries with two attempted murders in Vilnius connected to Russia's GRU military intelligence agency, marking a significant escalation in allegations of Russian intelligence operations on NATO territory.

headlines.headline: India and New Zealand Sign Major Free Trade Agreement
headlines.summary: India and New Zealand signed a comprehensive free trade agreement that will cut or eliminate tariffs on 95% of goods traded between them, as both nations seek to diversify trade relationships amid global disruptions from the Iran war and US tariff policies.

headlines.headline: Global Military Spending Surges to Record $2.9 Trillion
headlines.summary: World military expenditure reached an all-time high of $2.89 trillion in 2025, driven by Europe's rearmament push and rising tensions in Asia, marking the eleventh consecutive year of growth according to SIPRI data.

perspectives.angle: Global Military Spending Surges Despite US Ukraine Aid Freeze
perspectives.summary: Global military expenditure rose 2.9% in 2025, driven by defense spending increases in Europe and Asia, even as US military aid to Ukraine was frozen. The trend reflects growing security concerns worldwide.

headlines.headline: Japan PM Launches Defense Policy Review Panel Amid Rising Threats
headlines.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi inaugurated a panel to review national security and defense policies as tensions escalate with China, North Korea, and Russia in the Indo-Pacific region.

perspectives.angle: Japan Seeks Deeper Defense Ties with Australia Amid Indo-Pacific Tensions
perspectives.summary: Japan's Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi pledged to deepen defense cooperation with Australia at a Nikkei forum in Perth, citing an "increasingly severe" security environment in the Indo-Pacific as regional tensions mount.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy rejects EU associate membership offer, demands full accession by 2027
headlines.summary: Ukraine's president rejected a German-French proposal for associate membership at a Cyprus summit, insisting on full EU membership by 2027. German Chancellor Merz suggested Ukraine may need to accept territorial losses to advance EU prospects.

headlines.headline: US Pressures Taiwan Parliament to Pass Comprehensive Defense Budget
headlines.summary: The top U.S. diplomat in Taiwan urged the opposition-majority parliament to approve a comprehensive defense budget, emphasizing the critical need for integrated air and missile defense systems amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Italy Extradites Chinese Cyber-Espionage Suspect to US
headlines.summary: Italy has extradited Xu Zewei, a 34-year-old alleged Chinese state-sponsored hacker, to the US where he faces charges for stealing COVID-19 vaccine research during the pandemic as part of the "Hafnium" cyber-espionage campaign targeting universities and immunologists.

headlines.headline: 50+ Nations Launch First-Ever Fossil Fuel Exit Talks in Colombia
headlines.summary: More than 50 governments including major producers like Canada, Norway, Australia and Brazil are meeting in Santa Marta for the inaugural conference on phasing out fossil fuels, bypassing UN climate talks to address the main driver of global warming amid the Iran war energy crunch.

headlines.headline: Lebanese Minister Says Hezbollah Disarmament Must Happen
headlines.summary: Lebanon's Social Affairs Minister Haneen Sayed told a CSIS forum in Washington that Hezbollah disarmament "must happen" and is a government demand, but cautioned it would require a political settlement and time given the group's role as a "community" not just a militant organization.

headlines.headline: CATL Shares Drop 7% After Announcing $5 Billion Renewable Expansion Fund
headlines.summary: World's largest EV battery maker CATL announced a HK$39.2 billion (US$5 billion) private placement to expand its renewables business, with shares falling nearly 7% as investors reacted to the dilution amid global oil price surges from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Train Collision Kills 14 Near Jakarta
headlines.summary: A long-distance train crashed into a commuter train outside Jakarta, killing at least 14 people and injuring 84. Rescue teams worked to free passengers trapped in the women-only carriage. The accident raises fresh concerns about Indonesia's aging rail infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Indonesia Train Crash Kills 14, Including Women-Only Carriage Victims
perspectives.summary: A commuter train collision near Jakarta killed 14 people, mostly women in a women-only carriage, with 84 injured. President Prabowo ordered an investigation and admitted large parts of the rail network are poorly maintained.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Accused of Bombing Kunar University in Afghanistan
headlines.summary: Pakistani air strikes on Kunar province killed at least 7 people and wounded 75, including university students. Taliban condemned the attack as 'war crimes,' with reports of jets, drones, and mortars targeting civilian areas.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan Accused of Airstrikes on Afghan University
perspectives.summary: Taliban officials say Pakistani attacks on Kunar province killed at least 7 people and injured 75, including students at Kunar University. Pakistan denied the reports. The attacks threaten a fragile ceasefire brokered by China in April.

headlines.headline: Suspect in Washington Dinner Shooting Charged With Attempted Assassination of Trump
headlines.summary: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, was charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump after opening fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. He faces life in prison if convicted.

perspectives.angle: Suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump after correspondents dinner shooting
perspectives.summary: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, was charged with attempted assassination after allegedly trying to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner with weapons. He made his first federal court appearance.

headlines.headline: NATO Considers Ending Annual Summits Amid Trump Tensions
headlines.summary: Six sources told Reuters that NATO is weighing whether to end its practice of annual summits, potentially avoiding a tense encounter with Trump in his final year as questions mount over the alliance's future.

perspectives.angle: NATO considers ending annual summits amid tensions with Trump administration
perspectives.summary: Six sources tell Reuters the alliance may skip 2028 summit to avoid tense encounter in Trump final year, with some members pushing to hold summits every two years instead of annually.

headlines.headline: Afghanistan risks losing 25,000 female teachers and health workers by 2030
headlines.summary: UNICEF warns that Taliban restrictions on girls education and women employment could eliminate 25% of Afghanistan 2021 female workforce, affecting 1 million girls currently with projections to double by 2030.

headlines.headline: Regional police crackdown arrests 326 in Asia-wide child abuse probe
headlines.summary: Singapore, Brunei, Hong Kong, Japan, Malaysia, South Korea and Thailand conducted a four-week operation arresting 326 people and investigating 119 more across 382 locations for online child sexual exploitation.

headlines.headline: Zelensky orders 50,000 ground robots for Ukraine military
headlines.summary: Ukraine president announces major expansion of unmanned ground systems for logistics, evacuations and combat, following widespread drone adoption as the next step in modern warfare.

headlines.headline: Trump Administration Fires Entire National Science Board
headlines.summary: All 22 members of the National Science Board, the independent body overseeing the National Science Foundation, received termination notices from the Presidential Personnel Office. Scientists warn the move could enable deep cuts to fundamental research funding.

perspectives.angle: Trump Administration Fires Entire National Science Board
perspectives.summary: The Trump administration has terminated all 20+ members of the National Science Board, the governing body of the National Science Foundation, in a major shake-up of US science policy leadership.

headlines.headline: Taiwan on alert as Chinese warships approach Penghu Islands
headlines.summary: Taiwan detected a Chinese destroyer and frigate operating near the Penghu islands, home to major Taiwanese military bases, prompting Taipei to deploy naval and air forces to monitor the vessels.

headlines.headline: German Chancellor links Ukraine peace to territorial concessions
headlines.summary: Friedrich Merz suggested Ukraine may need to cede territory to Russia as part of a peace deal, framing such concessions as a potential pathway to EU membership for Kyiv.

headlines.headline: North Korea opens memorial for troops killed in Ukraine war
headlines.summary: Kim Jong Un inaugurated a memorial museum honoring North Korean soldiers killed fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, pledging continued support for Moscow's war effort during a ceremony attended by Russia's defense minister.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo meets first female Archbishop of Canterbury in historic encounter
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV prayed with Archbishop of Canterbury Sarah Mullally at the Vatican, vowing to overcome differences in a historic meeting with the first female leader of the Church of England and the global Anglican Communion.

headlines.headline: Bahrain strips 69 people of citizenship over Iran support
headlines.summary: Bahrain revoked citizenship of 69 people for what it called sympathy with Iran's hostile acts and collaboration with foreign entities. Rights groups condemned the move as a 'blatant abuse of power'.

perspectives.angle: Bahrain strips 69 people of citizenship over Iran sympathy during war
perspectives.summary: State news agency reports citizenship revocations include families of those accused of praising Iran hostile acts during the US-Israel conflict with Iran.

headlines.headline: Putin Pledges Support for Iran in St. Petersburg Talks with Foreign Minister Araghchi
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in St. Petersburg, offering support to navigate the "difficult period" while expressing hope for peace. Moscow has resisted being drawn into the Middle East conflict while continuing its invasion of Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Putin Hosts Iranian FM Araghchi, Pledges Support as US-Iran Talks Stall
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin hosted Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in Moscow, promising support as Iran faces pressure over the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The meeting comes amid failed US-Iran negotiations over ending the conflict.

headlines.headline: Lebanese President Aoun Rejects "Wars Serving Foreign Interests" in Rebuke to Hezbollah
headlines.summary: President Joseph Aoun pushed back against critics of negotiations with Israel, declaring that "traitors are those who drag Lebanon into a war to serve foreign interests" — a direct criticism of Hezbollah's wars "in support of Gaza and Iran" rather than Lebanon's interests.

headlines.headline: Australia Coalition Proposes $800M Plan to Double Fuel Reserves
headlines.summary: Australia's federal Coalition unveiled an $800 million plan to double the nation's minimum fuel reserves to 60 days, citing energy security concerns amid the Middle East conflict. The proposal adds at least one billion litres of new onshore storage with a focus on diesel.

headlines.headline: US reviews latest Iranian proposal to end war stalemate
headlines.summary: President Trump discussed a new Iranian proposal with his national security team as Tehran's foreign minister headed to Russia. The proposal reportedly offers to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for ending the US blockade.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo meets first female Archbishop of Canterbury in historic Vatican encounter
headlines.summary: Pope Leo and Archbishop Sarah Mullally met for the first time at the Vatican, exchanging gifts and praying together in a symbolic step toward unity between the Catholic Church and Church of England.

headlines.headline: IDF Chief Condemns Unethical Soldier Conduct as Discipline Erodes
headlines.summary: Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir warned that discipline has eroded in recent wars, citing incidents including soldiers smashing a Jesus statue in Lebanon and wearing unapproved patches.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Completes Major US Tank Deal With Final Abrams Shipment
headlines.summary: The acquisition marks the biggest upgrade to Taiwan armored forces in decades as it bolsters northern defense capabilities against Chinese threats.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan Receives Final Batch of M1A2T Abrams Tanks From US
perspectives.summary: Taiwan has received the last of its 108 M1A2T Abrams tanks from the United States, completing a .28 billion procurement deal that strengthens the island’s armored capabilities.
